core_mechanism: an environment-coupling-driven (dissipative) phase transition in which the competition between a coherent ordering coupling and a fluctuation-suppressing blockade term is tuned by coupling to a gapless dissipative bath, whose low-energy spectral weight shifts a sharp critical threshold separating two distinct macroscopic states
brief_reason: The abstract attributes the superconductor-to-insulator transition to the interplay of a coherent (Josephson) coupling and a suppressing (Coulomb-blockade) term, with coupling to a gapless bath enhancing fluctuations and exponentially shifting the sharp transition, which is exactly a dissipation-tuned competing-interaction phase transition.
